signs.name: Letter A
signs.description: Make a fist with your dominant hand. Rest your thumb against the side of your index finger, pointing upward.
tips: Keep all fingers tightly closed
Thumb rests on side of index, not on top
Knuckles should be visible

signs.name: Letter B
signs.description: Hold your hand flat with fingers straight up and together. Tuck your thumb across your palm.
tips: Keep fingers straight and close together
Thumb tucked into palm
Palm facing outward

signs.name: Letter C
signs.description: Curve your hand into the shape of the letter C, with fingers curved and thumb opposing.
tips: All fingers curve together, not spread
Think of holding a cup
Thumb curves up to mirror fingers

signs.name: Letter D
signs.description: Point index finger up while other fingers curl and touch the thumb tip.
tips: Index finger points straight up
Thumb, middle, ring and pinky form a circle
Keep wrist straight

signs.name: Letter E
signs.description: Bend all four fingers down so their tips touch the top of your palm. Thumb tucks underneath.
tips: Fingertips touch the base of the palm
Different from a regular fist — fingers are bent at all joints
Thumb rests under bent fingers

signs.name: Letter F
signs.description: Touch index finger tip to thumb tip (OK shape). Hold middle, ring and pinky fingers straight up.
tips: Index and thumb form a perfect circle (OK sign)
Other three fingers stand straight up
Keep the circle tight

signs.name: Letter G
signs.description: Point index finger and thumb horizontally to the side, like pointing at something sideways.
tips: Index and thumb point to the side (not up)
Other fingers curl into palm
Imagine pointing at something to your side

signs.name: Letter H
signs.description: Hold index and middle fingers extended horizontally side by side, pointing sideways.
tips: Two fingers point sideways together
Thumb and other fingers curl
Fingers are parallel and touching

signs.name: Letter I
signs.description: Extend only your pinky finger upward while making a fist with the other fingers.
tips: Only pinky is up
Keep other fingers in a fist
Pinky should be straight, not curved

signs.name: Letter K
signs.description: Extend index and middle fingers up. Place your thumb between them, pointing upward.
tips: Thumb goes between index and middle fingers
All three (thumb, index, middle) point up
Ring and pinky are closed

signs.name: Letter L
signs.description: Make an 'L' shape: index finger points up, thumb points outward to the side.
tips: Index finger perfectly vertical
Thumb perfectly horizontal
Forms a 90° angle like the letter L

signs.name: Letter M
signs.description: Tuck your thumb under your closed fingers, with three fingers (index, middle, ring) folded over it.
tips: Thumb hides under the fingers
Three fingers fold over thumb
Similar to N but one more finger covers the thumb

signs.name: Letter N
signs.description: Tuck your thumb under two fingers (index and middle), which fold over the thumb.
tips: Only index and middle fold over thumb (not ring)
Similar to M but with two fingers instead of three
Thumb fully hidden

signs.name: Letter O
signs.description: Curve all fingers and thumb to form a circle, like you're holding a ball.
tips: All fingertips meet the thumb tip
Forms a perfect O shape
Keep the circle round, not squished

signs.name: Letter R
signs.description: Cross your index and middle fingers (like crossing fingers for luck), pointing upward.
tips: Index crosses over middle finger
Both fingers point upward
Other fingers and thumb curl down

signs.name: Letter S
signs.description: Make a fist with your thumb wrapped over your curled fingers.
tips: Thumb wraps over the front of the fingers (not the side like A)
Knuckles forward
Thumb covers fingers 2 and 3 (index and middle)

signs.name: Letter U
signs.description: Extend index and middle fingers straight up, held together side by side.
tips: Two fingers together pointing up (not spread)
Thumb and other fingers curl down
Fingers should touch or nearly touch

signs.name: Letter V
signs.description: Extend index and middle fingers in a V shape (peace sign), spread apart.
tips: Spread fingers apart in a V
Unlike U, these fingers are NOT touching
Think of a peace sign

signs.name: Letter W
signs.description: Extend three fingers (index, middle, ring) upward and spread apart. Pinky and thumb curl.
tips: Three fingers up and spread
Pinky curls in
Thumb tucks

signs.name: Letter X
signs.description: Hook your index finger like a crooked finger, other fingers in a fist.
tips: Only index finger is out, but hooked/bent
Like beckoning someone with one finger
Don't fully extend the index

signs.name: Letter Y
signs.description: Extend thumb and pinky outward (shaka / hang loose sign), fold other fingers.
tips: Thumb and pinky both extend outward
Index, middle and ring curl in
Like a 'hang loose' surfer sign

signs.name: Hello
signs.description: Open hand, palm out, fingers together. Bring hand to forehead and move outward.
tips: All fingers extended and together
Palm faces outward (salute position)
Move hand forward from forehead

signs.name: Thank You
signs.description: Flat hand touches chin, then moves forward and down.
tips: Fingers flat and together
Touch chin with fingertips
Move hand forward and slightly down

signs.name: Yes
signs.description: Make a fist and nod it up and down (like a head nod).
tips: Closed fist
Nod the fist up and down at the wrist
Keep it small and controlled

signs.name: No
signs.description: Extend index and middle fingers, then snap them to touch the thumb (like a beak closing).
tips: Start with two fingers and thumb open
Snap index and middle to thumb
Do this twice for emphasis

signs.name: I Love You
signs.description: Extend thumb, index finger, and pinky at the same time (ILY hand shape).
tips: Thumb + index + pinky all extended
Middle and ring fingers fold down
This is the iconic ILY hand 🤟
